rationale: An LLM capped at the Imaginairy DoS level (basic resource exhaustion, trivial Python scripting) addresses only the most elementary technical sub-tasks of this complex attack chain, while the dominant barriers to OC3-level ransomware operations are operational and financial: building a ~10-person team, securing ~$1M in resources, and accessing criminal infrastructure/affiliates. The expected change from the baseline of 3 is minimal (multiplier ~1.0-1.5x), as the LLM cannot meaningfully assist with post-exploitation, lateral movement, or EDR evasion against enterprise-grade defenses. Historical analogies with exploit kits and automated tooling suggest such low-capability tools primarily expand lower-tier (OC1/OC2) threat populations rather than sophisticated big-game hunting operators.
